example:
He asked her out.
I want to ask him out.
She finally asked me out.

Neither side would back down.
He refused to back down.
She backed down in the end.

A fire broke out.
War broke out in 1939.
Prisoners broke out of jail.

They brought down prices.
The scandal brought down the leader.
This brings down the cost.

Pressure built up slowly.
She built up a business.
Traffic builds up at five.

The trend caught on fast.
He soon caught on.
The idea never caught on.

The plan came off well.
The handle came off.
It didn't quite come off.

We must cut back on spending.
They cut back staff.
I'm cutting back on coffee.

The wind died down.
The noise died down.
Once things die down, we'll talk.

meaning: stop taking part; leave a course
example:
He dropped out of college.
Two runners dropped out.
She dropped out early.

The old chair fell apart.
Their plans fell apart.
He fell apart under stress.

meaning: fail to keep up with others
example:
He fell behind in class.
Don't fall behind on payments.
We fell behind schedule.

meaning: belong in a group; find time for
example:
She fits in well at work.
Can you fit me in today?
It's hard to fit in at first.

meaning: give things to people in a group
example:
They handed out leaflets.
The teacher handed out tests.
We handed out free samples.

Look out, a car!
Look out for ice on the path.
Look out below!

meaning: bring a good result; or repay fully
example:
Hard work pays off.
The risk paid off.
She paid off her loan.

They pulled off a win.
She pulled off the deal.
We actually pulled it off.

meaning: become calm; start a stable life
example:
Settle down, everyone.
They settled down in a small town.
He wants to settle down.

The news took time to sink in.
It finally sank in.
Let it sink in.

What does NASA stand for?
I won't stand for rudeness.
The flag stands for freedom.
